steps.description: @assertion there's create and view on github button. and ui has no error

Perform a right-click on the "welcome" tab in the Visual Studio Code workspace, targeting the tab header area, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (301, 47) in the tab bar. This opens the context menu for tab-related actions such as closing, splitting, or managing the tab.
Click the "Close" option in the tab context menu of Visual Studio Code, located in the workspace area at coordinates (351, 63), to close the currently selected tab.
Click the "Create" button for the "Travel Engine Agent | M365 Copilot Retrieval API" sample in the "Featured samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension tab in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion sample preview pictures showed. and no error found.
Click the "Platform capability" dropdown in the Samples section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Bot (9)" option in the "Platform capability" dropdown menu within the Samples tab of Visual Studio Code, under the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Agent365" option in the "Tab" dropdown filter in the Samples section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Python" option in the "Language" dropdown filter within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Technology" dropdown menu in the Samples tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Adaptive Cards" option in the "Technology" dropdown filter menu within the Samples tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "SSO" checkbox option in the dropdown filter menu within the "Samples" gallery of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's no sample item showed.
Click the "clear" filter button next to active tags ("Tab x", "Python x", "SSO x") in the Samples gallery of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's samples shows in gallery

Click the search box in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface within Visual Studio Code, specifically targeting the text input field used for searching sample agents and solutions.
Type text: 'abcdefgh' into the search input field located in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's no sample item showed.
Click the search input field labeled "abcdefgh" in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit pane within Visual Studio Code.
Undo the text "abcdefgh" entered in the search bar within the Samples tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code using the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Z.

Undo the last action in the Visual Studio Code "Samples" workspace using the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Z, targeting the "Samples" tab displaying the Claude Agent with Agent365 SDK sample in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Undo the recent text entry in the "Samples" search bar (“abcde”) of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code using the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Z.
Undo the previous text entry in the search bar labeled "abcd" within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface in Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+Z.
@assertion there's samples shows in gallery
Click the "Language" dropdown in the Samples section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface within Visual Studio Code to filter featured samples by programming language.
Click the "Close (Ctrl+W)" button on the "Samples" tab header in Visual Studio Code to close the tab.
Click the "View Samples" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome sidebar of Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the highlighted crosshair.
@assertion there's create and view on github button. and ui has no error
Click the "Create" button for the "Trial Agent" sample in the Featured samples section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's sample page readme opened correctly, and picture shows correctly and no error.
Click the "Create" button in the Travel Agent sample interface within Visual Studio Code’s Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.

Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Choose the folder where your project root folder will be located" dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code workspace, specifically for setting the project directory path.
Click the "README.md" file in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code within the "TRAVEL-AGENT" workspace to select or open the file.
